source.submodule: Bitwise Operators - 2 (NOT, LEFT and Right Shifts)

topicTags: Bitwise Operators - 2 (NOT, LEFT and Right Shifts)

content.problemStatement:
As, $1 << B$ is $2^B$ or $100..$(B times)$..00$ in binary, we can use this to change bits of a number individually.

Note that, we will reference the last bit or Least Significant bit as the $0$-th bit here, second last bit as the $1$-st bit and so on.

Check if $B$-th bit is set. \
$A$ $\&$ $(1 << B)$ will have a non-zero value if the $B$-th bit of $A$ is set. \
Since $1 << B$ only has a single bit set at $B$-th position, the Bitwise And will give non-zero result only if the $B$-th bit of $A$ is set.

Example
```
1101010 (= 106)
106 & (1 << 2) = 1101010 & 100 = 0 (Zero value as the 2-nd bit is unset)

1101010 (= 106)
106 & (1 << 5) = 1101010 & 100000 = 100000 (Non-zero value as the 5-th bit is set)
```

Similarly we can use the shift operator to
- Flip $B$-th bit of a number - $A$ ^ $(1 << B)$
- Set $B$-th bit of a number - $A$ | $(1 << B)$

### Task
Read and run the code in the IDE. Also change the value of variables and check the verdict.
constraints:
- $1 \leq T \leq 1000$
- $2 \leq N \leq 10^5$
- $1 \leq A_i \leq 10^9$
- The sum of $N$ across all test cases does not exceed $10^5$
content.inputFormat:
- The first line of input will contain a single integer $T$, denoting the number of test cases. The description of the test cases follows.
- The first line of each test case contains a single integer $N$, denoting the length of array $A$.
- The second line of each test case contains $N$ space-separated integers $A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_N$ — denoting the array $A$.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a new line the maximum value of $A_1+A_N$ you can get after several right rotations.
